one_line_ask: Prep the supplied Verdafix product photos and hazard/barcode graphics, then run the client's authored InDesign master label template through a data-merge against a per-SKU/variant CSV to produce one print-ready CMYK-with-bleed PDF per label variant.
brief: Verdafix Industrie GmbH, a European maker of construction sealants and adhesives, is converting its product-label artwork from legacy flat PDFs into a maintainable, print-ready system. The team has already rebuilt ONE master label into an authored InDesign template (verdafix_label_master.indd) - a 90 x 130 mm bottle/canister wrap laid out with 3 mm bleed, carrying real InDesign data-merge fields (<<product_name>>, <<variant>>, <<net_content>>, <<sku>>, <<batch_prefix>>, <<h_statements>>, <<p_statements>>, <<usage_de>>) plus image frames for the product shot, the GHS hazard pictogram, and the EAN barcode. What they need now is production, not design: bind the master to the full product range and output one press-ready file per SKU/variant. Supplied assets: a variants CSV (verdafix_label_variants.csv, one row per SKU/variant across the MS-Polymer, Hybrid and Acryl ranges in 290 ml and 600 ml sizes - 12 rows total) whose column headers exactly match the merge fields and which references each variant's product-photo and barcode filenames; a folder of raw product photos shot on the line (slightly tilted, dull, cool-cast studio table shots that need leveling, tone and a consistent crop t
